chapter_title.ar: باب الشَّرْطِ فِي النِّكَاحِ وَ مَا يَجُوزُ مِنْهُ وَ مَا لَا يَجُوزُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 65 – The stipulation regarding the marriage, and what is allowed from it, and what is not allowed

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/145)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/145)
text: امْرَأَةٍ نَكَحَهَا رَجُلٌ فَأَصْدَقَتْهُ الْمَرْأَةُ وَ شَرَطَتْ عَلَيْهِ أَنَّ بِيَدِهَا الْجِمَاعَ وَ الطَّلَاقَ فَقَالَ خَالَفَ السُّنَّةَ وَ وَلَّى الْحَقَّ مَنْ لَيْسَ أَهْلَهُ وَ قَضَى أَنَّ عَلَى الرَّجُلِ الصَّدَاقَ وَ أَنَّ بِيَدِهِ الْجِمَاعَ وَ الطَّلَاقَ وَ تِلْكَ السُّنَّةُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, form Ibn Bukeyr, from one of our companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding a woman who got married to a man, so the woman kept a dower and stipulated upon him that the copulation and the divorce would be in her hands’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He would be opposing the Sunnah and he has placed the right upon the one who is not rightful for it, and the judgement is that upon the man is the dower, and that in his hand would be the copulation and the divorce, and that is the Sunnah’.
en.sarwar: 7. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Faddal from ibn Bukayr from certain persons of our people who have said the following: “About the case of a woman who marries a man and she as part of her mahr (dower) says that going to bed will be in her control as well as divorce, abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is against the Sunnah and has entrusted the right with one who is not the right person for it. Judgment requires that the man must pay mahr (dower). He has the right to go to bed and divorce and that is the Sunnah.’”
